metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1740
metadata.comment:
Rationale: 30/10/2017 Pim
- Data in Fig.4 shows PCDH17-Mediated Intercellular Homophilic Interaction and Fig.S5, 6, S6, 7 show effects on synapse development. I would propose to create one BP ticket based on this paper against 'synapse adhesion between pre- and post-synapse (GO:0099560)'. I do not see what stage of synapse development is affected, so I do not annotate this more specifically.

Fig.4: PCDH17-Mediated Intercellular Homophilic Interaction. Experiments show direct interaction of PCDH17 in trans (HEK cells) and co-localization with PCDH17 in co-cultured neurons. The adhesion term is derived from the know role of cadherins in cell adhesion.. Experimental description: Paper focussed on mouse brain lysate, brain section, slices and whole animal as indicated in the Figure legends (e.g. Figure 1 B "mouse brain lysate" and supplementary figures (e.g. Supplementary Figure 1 ."...in serial coronal sections of a P10 mouse") Primate brain section staining described in figure 8 but not used in the annotation.

30/10/2017 Pim
- Overexpressed recombinant protein in pull FC-puldown essays is human species: "To generate the PCDH17E-Fc fusion protein, the clone of human IgG1 Fc region was raised from standard RT-PCR and its cDNA"
- CHO cells express mouse Protocadherin-17
- Mouse species was selected based on KO data and species of overexpressed protein.
